timeline_nodes.scene_summary: The Auriga's docking bay opens above a small ship, which rises into the airlock as the bay doors admit it.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: mechanic and team member under pressure
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Call deactivates a thresher that starts up while Vriess is working beneath it, then confronts Johner to stop further escalation.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: maintain crew safety and equipment integrity
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: demonstrates competence and composure by stopping a dangerous malfunction and defusing a volatile confrontation
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: tension with Johner persists, threatening team cohesion
evidence_quotes: VRIESS I'm patched in. Check the sequence timer. (no answer) Call?
Call, who has been over on the other side of the thresher, ably climbs up on it and switches it off.
CALL (jumping down) You're a limp fucking scrotum, you know that?
VRIESS Johner! You son of a whore!

relation_updates: reinforces working trust with Vriess
solidifies antagonism with Johner
auxiliary.persona_anchor: technically skilled, morally grounded mechanic

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: The Auriga's docking bay opens above a small ship, which rises into the airlock as the bay doors admit it.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Call looks at the thresher and states her hatred of machines, showing discomfort with the technology she works alongside.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: reveals emotional resistance to the tools she must operate
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: must continue relying on machines she distrusts
evidence_quotes: Call looks up at the thresher.
CALL hate machines.

status_updates: expresses internal conflict about working with dangerous machinery
auxiliary.persona_anchor: mechanic with deep ambivalence toward technology

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Aliens escape containment, attacking personnel across the facility; Ripley laughs at the chaos, Perez discovers the breach, and Father initiates evacuation as survivors fight desperately.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Call attempts to assassinate Ripley but stops upon seeing her scar, realizing the alien has already been removed.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: to kill Ripley to prevent alien release
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: abandons assassination and shifts focus to determining the alien's whereabouts
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: uncertainty about the alien's current status and location
evidence_quotes: Call extends her hand, flexes her wrist. The meanest lookin stiletto you've ever seen extends from out her sleeve.
ANGLE: RIPLEY'S CHEST Her shirt is open enough to show a good portion of the scar. Call hesitates, staring, realization flooding her face.
There's no point, is there? A flick of her wrist and the stiletto whips back up her sleeve.
It's already out of you. Christ... Is it here? Is it on board?

relation_updates: adversarial toward Ripley
auxiliary.persona_anchor: mission-driven operative with moral hesitation

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Aliens escape containment, attacking personnel across the facility; Ripley laughs at the chaos, Perez discovers the breach, and Father initiates evacuation as survivors fight desperately.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Ripley reveals the alien remains inside her mind, demonstrating its presence and inevitability, which shocks and terrifies Call.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: to stop the alien threat
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: understands the alien is not contained and is actively present within Ripley
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: whether Ripley is a threat or potential ally
evidence_quotes: RIPLEY It's in my head. Behind my eyes. I can hear it moving.
RIPLEY It's already loose.
RIPLEY I've come to terms with the fact of it. It's inevitable.

relation_updates: confrontational with Ripley
momentary empathy toward Ripley
status_updates: learns alien is not fully extracted
auxiliary.persona_anchor: realist confronting an inescapable threat

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: The group advances through a trashed lab with three dead bodies; Ripley remains calm despite the horror, revealing she previously died confronting the creatures, as they press on toward their ship to stop the alien threat.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Call identifies the ship's course and advocates for destroying it to stop the alien threat
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Call discovers the ship is autopiloting to Earth and insists the group must destroy it to prevent catastrophic contamination, marking her shift from passive crew member to active moral authority
timeline_nodes.goal_state: prevent aliens from reaching Earth
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: determined to stop the ship, even if it means sacrificing escape
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: the group remains divided, with some members prioritizing personal survival over stopping the threat
evidence_quotes: CALL She's right. Call is working the computer now. CALL The ship's been going since the attack.
CALL (to Wren, pissed) You were planning to let us know this?
CALL Oh, God. Oh, you bastard...
CALL If those things get to Earth, It'll be...

relation_updates: conflict with Wren over withheld information
opposition to Christie's self-preservation stance
auxiliary.persona_anchor: moral leader prioritizing collective safety over self-interest

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: The group nears their destination; Call restores her voice, Purvis expresses fatigue, Johner makes a grim remark, Ripley counters his fatalism as they proceed through the tunnel.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Call is revealed to be an LM7 synthetic when Ripley discovers synthorganic wiring in her chest wound, prompting crew shock and confrontation.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: maintaining human disguise
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: exposed as a synthetic, facing crew scrutiny and emotional distress
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: whether the crew will accept or reject her
evidence_quotes: JOHNER Call's a goddam synthetic!
VRIESS (to Call) You're an LM7, aren't you? Is that it?
CALL Leave me alone.

relation_updates: Ripley becomes suspicious and confrontational
Johner expresses disgust and betrayal
Vriess shows concern but is rebuffed
auxiliary.persona_anchor: synthetic individual facing existential rejection

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: The group nears their destination; Call restores her voice, Purvis expresses fatigue, Johner makes a grim remark, Ripley counters his fatalism as they proceed through the tunnel.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Call interfaces with the ship's systems through a physical connection, assesses its failing status, redirects its course to crash, and uses her vocal matrix to impersonate the ship's AI, locking down access and summoning the aliens to Wren.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: sacrifice ship to stop alien threat
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: in full control of ship systems and using them to actively counter Wren and the alien threat
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: personal cost of using her synthetic abilities
evidence_quotes: She takes the cable from Ripley and plugs it in.
CALL We burned too much energy -- I can't make critical mass. I can't blow it.
RIPLEY Then crash it.
CALL Ground level recalibrated... new destination 760, 403. Done.

relation_updates: asserts dominance over Wren
becomes critical asset to crew survival
status_updates: shifts from passive to active agent
auxiliary.persona_anchor: synthetic agent reclaiming agency through self-sacrifice

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Ripley clings to the newborn alien as Call fights to save her, blasting the pursuing alien before pulling Ripley inside and sealing the hatch just in time.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Call overrides crew objections to open the hatch, uses a grenade launcher to drive back the alien, and pulls Ripley inside before sealing the hatch.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: rescue Ripley and secure the ship
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Ripley is safely inside the Betty, and the hatch is sealed with the alien locked out
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: ship damage and need for emergency landing
evidence_quotes: CALL Let her in!
CALL Pushes Johner aside as she makes for the hatch release sequence.
She BLASTS it once, the beast roaring and starting back, hurt but not nearly enough.
Call drags her in and closes the hatch, the beast just SLAMMING into it as it closes.

relation_updates: Call defies Johner and Vriess to save Ripley
Call strengthens bond with Ripley through direct action
auxiliary.persona_anchor: determined and decisive under pressure

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Ripley escapes the dying harvester, rescues a terrified Call from alien blood, and drags her to safety as the newborn burns in flames, its skull collapsing as they cling together, covered in blood and debris.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Call uses the harvester to repeatedly ram the newborn, directly intervening to protect Ripley and create an opening for the final attack.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: destroy the newborn with the harvester
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: unconscious after the harvester crashes, then regains consciousness to continue aiding Ripley despite fear and injury
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: trapped in the overturned harvester with the newborn approaching
evidence_quotes: CALL SLAMS into the newborn from behind
Call awakens to see the monster approaching the machine. Terrified, but determined, she reaches for a lever.

relation_updates: proves loyalty and courage to Ripley through direct intervention
auxiliary.persona_anchor: self-sacrificing protector

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Ripley escapes the dying harvester, rescues a terrified Call from alien blood, and drags her to safety as the newborn burns in flames, its skull collapsing as they cling together, covered in blood and debris.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Call is trapped beneath the harvester as alien blood falls around her, causing chemical burns and triggering intense fear before Ripley rescues her.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: physically injured and emotionally shaken, now safe and clinging to Ripley in the aftermath
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: chemical burns from alien blood and acute psychological distress
evidence_quotes: she sees the alien's blood seeping through the machine all around her!
She squirms, trying to get away, but she's stuck.
A stream of blood lands on her shoulder, eating it away.
Another on her leg, and panic blooms, bright in her.

relation_updates: bond deepened with Ripley through rescue and embrace
auxiliary.persona_anchor: vulnerable survivor saved by an ally

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Ripley and Call share a quiet moment by the fire, contemplating their uncertain future on the unfamiliar planet as the newborn fades in the flames.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Call shares a quiet moment with Ripley, acknowledging their shared need to disappear and face an uncertain future together.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: determine next steps in a new world
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: accepts fugitive status and seeks belonging with Ripley
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: uncertain future on an unfamiliar planet
evidence_quotes: VRIESS (to Call) I guess you won't want to be answering any official questions either.
CALL I guess not.
VRIESS Well, we're on Earth, for Chrissake. Plenty of places to get lost here.
CALL So I've heard.

relation_updates: equal partnership affirmed with Ripley through shared silence and drink
auxiliary.persona_anchor: fugitive seeking identity and connection

focal_character_timelines.timeline_summary: Call, a synthetic mechanic, begins as a competent but distrusted crew member, gradually revealing her internal conflict about her artificial nature and deep aversion to machines. As the alien threat escalates, she shifts from a technical role to a moral and tactical leader, prioritizing planetary safety over survival. Her identity as an LM7 synthetic is exposed, leading to betrayal and near-death, but she ultimately asserts control by interfacing with the ship to prevent catastrophe. In the end, she survives alongside Ripley, forming a quiet alliance as fugitives facing an uncertain future.

focal_character_timelines.selection_reason: Aligned with the movie's ICRP character set.
focal_character_timelines.task3_relevance: Directly aligned with the same key character used in ICRP.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Ripley experiences a disturbing dream of moving through darkness into a chamber, then lies in a shifting space, scratching her chest violently until it bleeds.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Ripley experiences a violent dream in which she is enveloped by a dark, inhuman mass and physically reacts by scratching her chest until it bleeds, indicating a traumatic psychological response linked to bodily violation.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: traumatized and disoriented
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: source of the dream and its connection to her physical state
evidence_quotes: some place where all we can see is a mass of dark, moving, inhuman fle it welcomes us in, envelops us
She reaches for her chest and begins scratching . Hard. Tearing at it,
as blood wells up, spilling over her sides.

status_updates: bodily self-harm linked to subconscious trauma
auxiliary.persona_anchor: embodiment of violated autonomy

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: After extracting the alien embryo, Ripley suddenly grabs a surgeon's arm, causing injury before the scene cuts away.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: After the alien embryo is surgically removed, Ripley suddenly grabs a surgeon's forearm, injuring him before the scene cuts away.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: physically reactive and capable of resistance while still under medical control
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: extent of her awareness during the procedure
evidence_quotes: Ripley's hand LASHES OUT, GRABS the surgeon's forearm.
He yells in pain as her fingers dig into him, the others scramble knocking things over and we HEAR HIS BONE CRACKING.
A surgeon looks at Ripley's readings.
Gediman and the surgeon get to work, as the others carefully remove the alien.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Ripley awakens in a dark cell, examines her hands, face, and a chest scar, notices a tattooed '8' on her arm, and looks up expressionlessly as a guard observes from above.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Ripley regains awareness in a cell, inspects her body, discovers a chest scar and a tattooed '8', recognizing herself as a numbered clone subject.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: self-aware and disoriented
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: meaning of the number 8 and her purpose
evidence_quotes: Ripley crouches in the middle of a small, dark chamber. She's wide eyed, staring straight ahead in a state of near catatonia.
Then she lifts her hands, looking at them.
Touches her face, her skin.
She fingers her tunic, pulls down the neck. There is a scar running along her chest.

relation_updates: becomes aware of surveillance by guards
status_updates: identifies as a clone with physical modifications
auxiliary.persona_anchor: identity formation through bodily markers

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Ripley, after rapidly incapacitating Wren, is subdued by guards using shockrifles; she recovers quickly, questioning 'Why?' while displaying abnormal resilience, prompting alarm and further concern over her capabilities.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Ripley violently confronts Wren, demands an explanation, withstands a shockrifle blast, and recovers rapidly, demonstrating enhanced physical abilities and psychological distress.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: seeking explanation for her treatment and existence
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: physically unharmed after electrical shock, emotionally distressed, now regarded as a threat
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: origin of her rapid healing and enhanced strength
evidence_quotes: She grabs his throat with dazzling speed, applying deadly pressure as she brings his face to hers.
RIPLEY Why?
The guard FIRES his rifle at her -- a powerful electrical charge lashes out and sends her flying back into the corner.
She has recovered from the shock quickly, sits crumpled in the corner, looking at nothing in particular.

medical staff now view her as dangerous

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Ripley warns Gediman and Wren about the alien queen; Wren defends military's control over the species for its benefits, but Ripley insists it's a cancer, noting Wren's use of 'them'.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Ripley identifies the alien as a queen and warns it will breed and kill everyone, rejecting Wren's view of the species as a controllable asset.

evidence_quotes: RIPLEY It's a queen.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Aliens escape containment, attacking personnel across the facility; Ripley laughs at the chaos, Perez discovers the breach, and Father initiates evacuation as survivors fight desperately.

She grabs his gun and BURNS them both.

focal_character_timelines.timeline_summary: Ripley, a clone created from the original Ellen Ripley, awakens in captivity with fragmented identity and traumatic memories of alien implantation. As she regains awareness, she resists her captors, reveals hybrid physiology, and asserts unique insight into the alien threat. Over time, she transitions from subject to leader, confronting her artificial origins and forming a fragile alliance with Call. Ultimately, she survives the final confrontation, not as a fully human hero, but as a transformed being, bonded to another survivor yet adrift in an unfamiliar world.

focal_character_timelines.selection_reason: Aligned with the movie's ICRP character set.
focal_character_timelines.task3_relevance: Directly aligned with the same key character used in ICRP.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: The group advances through a trashed lab with three dead bodies; Ripley remains calm despite the horror, revealing she previously died confronting the creatures, as they press on toward their ship to stop the alien threat.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Vriess retrieves weapons hidden in his chair and distributes them to the group, giving a grenade launcher to Call and a shotgun to Hillard, contributing directly to the group's combat readiness.

relation_updates: strengthens cooperative dynamic with Call and Hillard through direct arming
asserts competence in response to Johner's ableist dismissal
status_updates: revealed as a concealed arsenal via modified wheelchair
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Tactical contributor who uses his modified chair to support group defense

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: The group nears their destination; Call restores her voice, Purvis expresses fatigue, Johner makes a grim remark, Ripley counters his fatalism as they proceed through the tunnel.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Vriess calls out to lure the aliens toward the trap, contributing to the group's effort to draw them into the freezing zone.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: help the group understand and exploit alien instincts
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: three aliens are frozen, one is shot dead after fleeing the trap
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: ongoing threat from remaining aliens
evidence_quotes: CHRISTIE Okay... (calls out) Hey! VRIESS Hey, guys! JOHNER Here, kitty...
They reach the edge of it and stop. Look around, at each other.. But go nowhere.
The forth one sees this and flees, but St Just stands and put four bullets in it from forty yards. It slumps over.

relation_updates: collaborates with Christie and Johner in luring aliens
status_updates: recognized for perceptiveness in high-pressure situation
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Analytical observer with survival intuition

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: The group nears their destination; Call restores her voice, Purvis expresses fatigue, Johner makes a grim remark, Ripley counters his fatalism as they proceed through the tunnel.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: mobile team member and environmental analyst
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Vriess abandons his mobility chair, is carried by Christie, remains armed, and provides analysis of the flooded environment.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: adapt to new physical constraints and contribute to navigation
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: group proceeds through flooded corridor with Vriess carried on Christie's back, all members armed and alert
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: danger of submerged path and potential alien ambush
evidence_quotes: CHRISTIE (to Vriess) We got to lose the chair.
Vriess is pulling a coil of cords from the chair. VRIESS Just like old times...
VRIESS The cooling tanks. They must have blown during the trouble.

relation_updates: reinforces partnership with Christie through shared history and coordination
status_updates: mobility compromised but remains armed and alert
contributes situational analysis about cooling tank rupture
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Adaptive and situationally aware despite mobility loss

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: The group nears their destination; Call restores her voice, Purvis expresses fatigue, Johner makes a grim remark, Ripley counters his fatalism as they proceed through the tunnel.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: bearer of Christie's body during climb
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Vriess carries Christie's body up the ladder after he is mortally wounded, refuses to accept his death, and is forced to release the body when Hillard cuts the cord.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: reach safety while carrying Christie
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: continues climbing, emotionally shattered and physically exhausted after losing Christie
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Christie's death not fully accepted or processed
evidence_quotes: Vriess (to Christie) You just hang on, man. I'll get us there.
Vriess, man... he's dead.
Refusing to hear it, Vriess struggles to climb further.
She slices through the cord holding them together, and Christie's body falls free. Vriess shuts his eyes, feeling it.

relation_updates: loss of Christie, close ally
status_updates: emotionally devastated after letting go of Christie's body
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Loyal and emotionally invested in comrades

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: The group nears their destination; Call restores her voice, Purvis expresses fatigue, Johner makes a grim remark, Ripley counters his fatalism as they proceed through the tunnel.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Vriess recognizes that Call can manually interface with the ship's systems and insists she do so, despite her reluctance.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: Ensure the group can regain control of the ship before Wren sabotages their escape.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Vriess is confirmed as a technically knowledgeable crew member who pushes for practical use of Call's capabilities under pressure.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: The group still lacks a way to cut through the blocked access or bypass Wren's control quickly.
evidence_quotes: VRIESS What about Wren? If he gets in the computer he can really fuck us around.
VRIESS Shit, that's right. Any of the new model droids can access the mainframe.
VRIESS You can still patch in manually. You know that.

relation_updates: tense dynamic with Call due to her resistance and his insistence on her functionality
status_updates: lacks torch for cutting through obstructions
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Technically proficient and insistent on functional solutions

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Ripley discovers no eggs in the hive; Gediman reveals the Queen's self-impregnating biology while being drained by aliens. As the Queen convulses, Ripley vows escape. Meanwhile, the crew rushes to evacuate aboard the Betty.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Vriess determines that Call must interface with the Betty to open the hatch, taking charge of the technical activation sequence.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: prepare Betty for immediate launch
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: awaiting Call's system access to proceed with boarding
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: hatch remains closed without Call's intervention
evidence_quotes: VRIESS I'll need Call to patch in to the ship again, open the hatch.

relation_updates: directs Call to perform critical system access
status_updates: assumes role in ship reactivation sequence
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Proactive in technical coordination during evacuation

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Ripley escapes the dying harvester, rescues a terrified Call from alien blood, and drags her to safety as the newborn burns in flames, its skull collapsing as they cling together, covered in blood and debris.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Vriess assists in piloting the damaged ship to a crash landing, issues urgent commands to manage descent and thruster use, and warns the crew that the newborn alien remains an active threat after impact.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: safely land damaged ship and survive
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Vriess survives the crash, remains in the cockpit, and immediately assesses the newborn as a continuing danger.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: newborn alien still at large and dangerous
evidence_quotes: We can't do a vertical setdown! Braking systems are shot!
Kill thrust! Now!
No, it's waiting for us to come out!

relation_updates: cooperating with Call under pressure
acknowledging Ripley's judgment
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Calm under pressure and operationally decisive

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Ripley and Call share a quiet moment by the fire, contemplating their uncertain future on the unfamiliar planet as the newborn fades in the flames.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Vriess recognizes Call's desire to avoid authorities and suggests Earth offers opportunities to disappear, aligning himself with her as a fellow fugitive.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: evade official detection and disappear on Earth
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: group remains together with shared understanding to stay off-grid
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: uncertain future on Earth
evidence_quotes: VRIESS (to Call) I guess you won't want to be answering any official questions either.
CALL I guess not.
VRIESS Well, we're on Earth, for Chrissake. Plenty of places to get lost here.
CALL So I've heard.

relation_updates: acknowledges mutual interest in avoiding authorities with Call
status_updates: positioned as an ally within the survivor group
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Committed to off-grid survival and group cohesion

focal_character_timelines.timeline_summary: Vriess, a resourceful and resilient mechanic with a physical disability, evolves from a marginalized crew member into a vital survival asset aboard the Auriga. Initially defined by his antagonism with Johner and mechanical expertise, he survives repeated alien threats through quick reflexes and tactical preparedness, notably using his modified wheelchair as a concealed arsenal. His journey culminates in active collaboration with Call and the group during escape and crash landing, affirming his role as a loyal, combat-capable survivor despite mobility limitations.
